2605.05632.title: Architecture Matters: Comparing RAG Systems under Knowledge Base Poisoning
2605.05632.paper_summary: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems are vulnerable to knowledge base poisoning, yet existing attacks have been evaluated almost exclusively against vanilla retrieve-then-generate pipelines. Architectures designed to handle conflicting retrieved information - multi-agent debate, agentic retrieval, recursive language models - remain untested against adversarially optimized contradictions. We evaluate four RAG architectures (vanilla RAG, agentic RAG, MADAM-RAG, and Recursive Language Models) under controlled single-document (N=1) poisoning on 921 Natural Questions QA pairs, comparing a clean baseline, naive injection, and CorruptRAG-AK - an adversarial attack whose meta-epistemic framing targets credibility assessment. Architecture is a high-impact variable in adversarial robustness: under CorruptRAG-AK, attack success rates range from 81.9% (vanilla) to 24.4% (RLM) - a spread of nearly 58 percentage points across architectures with comparable clean accuracy (~92%). Decomposing this gap, once the poisoned document is retrieved, adversarial framing - not retrieval optimization - drives the majority of CorruptRAG-AK's advantage for three of four architectures, localizing the cross-architecture vulnerability at the content-reasoning stage. Our MADAM-RAG reimplementation shows the highest apparent contradiction detection rate, though our LLM judge over-identifies this behavior (~48.5% precision), so reported rates are upper bounds. Regardless of detection, MADAM-RAG cannot resolve contradictions reliably, producing a 41.4% non-answer rate even on clean inputs - though implementation divergences from the original may contribute. We introduce a seven-category behavioral taxonomy capturing contradiction detection, hedging, and failure modes beyond binary accuracy. Code, data, and analysis notebooks are publicly available.
2605.05632.paper_summary_zh: 檢索增強生成（RAG）系統易受到知識庫中毒的影響，但現有的攻擊幾乎僅針對原始的檢索-然後-生成管道進行評估。旨在處理衝突檢索信息的架構——多代理辯論、代理檢索、遞歸語言模型——尚未針對對抗性優化的矛盾進行測試。我們在921個自然問題問答對上，對四種RAG架構（原始RAG、代理RAG、MADAM-RAG和遞歸語言模型）進行了受控的單文檔（N=1）中毒評估，並比較了乾淨基線、天真注入和CorruptRAG-AK——一種對抗性攻擊，其元認識框架針對信譽評估。架構是對抗穩健性中的一個高影響變量：在CorruptRAG-AK下，攻擊成功率範圍從81.9%（原始）到24.4%（RLM）——在具有可比乾淨準確度（約92%）的架構中，差距近58個百分點。分解這一差距後，一旦檢索到中毒文檔，對抗性框架——而非檢索優化——驅動了CorruptRAG-AK在四種架構中三種的優勢，將跨架構的脆弱性定位在內容推理階段。我們的MADAM-RAG重新實現顯示出最高的明顯矛盾檢測率，儘管我們的LLM判斷者過度識別了這一行為（約48.5%的精確度），因此報告的比率是上限。無論檢測如何，MADAM-RAG無法可靠地解決矛盾，即使在乾淨輸入上也產生41.4%的無答案率——儘管與原始實現的差異可能會有所貢獻。我們引入了一個七類行為分類法，捕捉矛盾檢測、對沖和超越二元準確度的失敗模式。代碼、數據和分析筆記本公開可用。
2605.05632.author: Samuel Korn et.al.
